title: Luwingo
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Luwingo_swenc.jpg]] |name=Luwingo |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Yaka]]''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber=[[Cyborg/Legends|Cyborg]] [[Brain/Legends|brain]] with Yaka intelligence modifications |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Ntaobh. Hck hk.|Luwingo|[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]]}} '''Luwingo''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Yaka]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]. Like others of his [[Species/Legends|species]], Luwingo possessed a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[Brain/Legends|brain]] and was implanted with intelligence modifications that greatly enhanced his mental abilities. He originally worked as a [[Programmer/Legends|programmer]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] until he saved up enough money to pay for a [[Galaxy Tours/Legends|Galaxy Tours]] cruise for both himself and his family. During the cruise, the [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liner]] was hijacked by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] who kidnapped Luwingo's family, intending to sell them into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. Luwingo dedicated the rest of his life to searching for his family by becoming a smuggler, believing the profession to be the best means of traveling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. He soon came across a fellow smuggler, [[Lo Khan]], and the two became partners. Luwingo saved Khan's life from a rival group of smugglers, cementing the beginning of a long-running friendship between the two partners. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Luwingo and Khan were stranded on the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] after Khan's [[Transport/Legends|transport]], the ''[[Hyperspace Marauder]]'', was impounded by [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]. [[Standard month/Legends|Months]] later, Luwingo and Khan aided a group of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] in retreating from a failed attempt to [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|attack]] the [[Emperor's Citadel]]. The two smugglers were able to break their [[Starship/Legends|ship]] out of the impound and escape Byss. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|Keep it to yourself, but ''Luwingo'' here saved my can in a shootout with Imperials on the Gamor Run. I keep him around for ''protection''.|Lo Khan, to Shug Ninx|}} Luwingo was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Yaka]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]] commonly modified with intelligence implants by the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanians]] centuries before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Luwingo worked as a [[Programmer/Legends|programmer]] on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], developing software and programs for commercial use. He was eventually able to save up enough money to go on a luxury cruise on [[Galaxy Tours/Legends|Galaxy Tours]] with his family. During the cruise, a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] group attacked the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] everyone on-board with [[Stun gas|knockout gas]]. As the pirates looted the ship's vaults and guest rooms, they noticed Luwingo's children and kidnapped them, intending to sell them into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] for their enhanced [[Brain/Legends|brains]], and kidnapped the rest of Luwingo's family as well. Luwingo decided to go after the pirates and rescue his family by becoming a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], believing the profession to be the most effective method of traveling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. After procuring a ship with some back pay, Luwingo quit his job and began smuggling. He began noticing how odd of a figure he made smuggling by himself, and when his funds began running low he decided to find a partner to blend in better with the other smugglers. He decided to seek out [[Lo Khan]], a smuggler he had met on an earlier run. As Luwingo began to research Khan's whereabouts, he discovered that the smuggler was being set up by one of his rivals, [[Uxbeg]]. In the [[Deneba system/Legends|Deneba system]], Luwingo ambushed and eliminated Uxbeg's henchmen before they could attack Khan. Luwingo soon ran into Khan, who took pity on the Yaka due to his desperate state of affairs, and offered him a job as his temporary partner. Another group of thugs attacked the pair, resulting in a [[Lightfight|firefight]] in which Luwingo took a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt for Khan. Though Khan was worried about Luwingo's well-being, the Yaka recovered almost immediately. The incident cemented a friendship between the smugglers, and Luwingo became Khan's full-time [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]]. ===Aiding the New Republic=== {{Quote|Hdoogkva!|Luwingo's battle cry|[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]]}} [[File:LuwingoGoesRambo.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Luwingo provides covering fire for the other smugglers.]] Luwingo spent the next few [[Galactic Standard Calendar|years]] trying to track down his family between smuggling runs. Luwingo and Khan began [[Deep Core hauler|hauling supplies]] to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], and in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], they were present at the [[Byss Bistro]] inside Byss's [[Imperial Freight Complex]]. Fellow smugglers [[Salla Zend]] and [[Shug Ninx]], on the run from the Empire after they helped smuggle their friends—[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]—into the [[Emperor's Citadel]], requested aid from Luwingo and Khan. The pair allowed Zend and Ninx to store Solo's [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], inside Khan's [[Transport/Legends|transport]], the ''[[Hyperspace Marauder]]'', and proceeded to discuss recent events inside the Bistro. A [[Hunter-Killer probot]] designated [[Judgement 12-X7]] soon detected the ''Falcon'' inside the ''Marauder'' and disabled the ship, leaving Luwingo and Khan stranded on Byss for several [[Standard month/Legends|months]]. Luwingo and Khan met up with Zend and Ninx again at the Bistro months later. They watched as a shipment of newly arrived [[X-1 Viper]] [[Battle droid/Legends|war droids]], secretly harboring [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]], began [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|wreaking havoc]] on the Imperials.[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] However, the New Republic forces were soon outmatched when the Imperials released their [[Chrysalide]] beasts. Realizing the trouble the New Republic forces were in, Zend rallied together a group of smugglers, including Luwingo and Khan, to aid the commandos. As the smugglers ran for their ships, Luwingo laid down covering fire against Imperial forces with his [[tripod laser]]. Once the other smugglers safely boarded their freighters, Luwingo joined Khan in liberating the ''Marauder'' from the Imperial impound and proceeded to help the New Republic invasion force in its retreat. Luwingo and Khan joined the rest of the newly formed smuggler [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] and fled Byss to [[Pinnacle Base]], headquarters of the New Republic. Luwingo and Khan, in conjunction with the smuggler fleet, later helped [[Battle of New Alderaan (Operation Shadow Hand)|evacuate]] the New Republic [[Safe world/Legends|safe world]] of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]] before finally heading off to the New Republic base at the [[Nespis VIII|Space City]] above [[Nespis VIII]].[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Make sure the probes don't see ya takin' off from the ''Marauder''. That ship's all Luwingo and me got! Wingo'd be ''heartbroken'' if somethin' happened to her!''"
"''Chra glg odwogn!|Lo Khan and Luwingo, to Salla Zend and Shug Ninx|}} [[File:LuwingoEGPM.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Luwingo]] A Yaka, Luwingo had a cyborg brain with many implants and mind enhancements that made him, like others of his species, one of the more intelligent and technically deft individuals in the galaxy. These intelligence modifications had the unexpected side-effect of leaving Luwingo and the other Yaka with a twisted view of life and a bizarre sense of humor. He spent much of his [[Time/Legends|time]] thinking about hyperdimensional equations or developing new strategies for beating an [[L7 logician droid|L7 Logician]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]], a task Luwingo never found particularly challenging because he was often able to beat the droid with ease.''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' This left Luwingo bored most of the time, reflecting back on his life and how it had not turned out as well as he had hoped, but at the same time could have ended up much worse. Strong and bulky, Luwingo was also a very imposing figure and could survive being [[Laser/Legends|shot]] by a blaster. He formed a close relationship with Lo Khan and served as the smuggler's bodyguard and partner. After the incident aboard the [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury ship]] that resulted in the kidnapping of his family, Luwingo dedicated his life to tracking down his family and bringing the pirates to justice. He knew the memories of the pirate attack would impair his investigation, so he typically kept them out of his mind by reprogramming his cyborg brain. Only one thing made Luwingo feel good inside: thinking about what he would do to the pirates once he found them. Though Khan tried to navigate among the safest [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] whenever possible, he and Luwingo had a system for dealing with potential hijackers if they ran into trouble. If any hijackers were able to board the ''Hyperspace Marauder'', they would have to confront Luwingo, and if they attempted to reach for a weapon, Luwingo would become angry. If the hijackers tried to tow the ship or cut into the cargo bay, Luwingo would have enough time to [[Slicer/Legends|slice]] into their ship's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] systems and shut it down. Luwingo and Khan both had a strong emotional attachment to the ''Marauder'', so much so that they considered the transport to be the only valuable possession they owned between them. Luwingo was proficient in computer programming, droid programming, and repair of computers, droids, and [[Realspace/Legends|space]] transport vessels. Although he spoke a [[Language/Legends|language]] quite different from [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], he could understand conversations spoken in the galactic standard. ==Behind the scenes== Luwingo made his first appearance in the [[Dark Empire 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]]. He later appeared in the latter half of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' and was given a full background in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' by [[Michael Allen Horne]]. He was also mentioned in several [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|''Essential Guides'']] and a number of other reference sources, most recently ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. Though the fifth issue of ''Dark Empire'' indicates that Luwingo saved Khan during a firefight against Imperials, the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' instead identifies the attackers as hirelings of Uxbeg. This article assumes that the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'', being the most recent and comprehensive source on the subject, is correct. In [[Dark Empire II 4|issue four]] of ''Dark Empire II'', Luwingo says, "Namym si niwd oog hcra!," which, when read backwards, says, "Arch Goodwin is my man." This is likely a reference to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' comic book writer [[Archie Goodwin]]. ==Appearances== *[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] *[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]] *[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] {{C|Indirect appearance}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Luwingo}} *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{C|Under ''Shug Ninx'' and ''Salla Zend''}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' {{C|Under ''Hyperspace Marauder''}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' {{C|Under ''Arkania''}} *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{FactFile|93|BYS 1-2|Byss}} {{Po}} *{{FactFile|116|CAL 19-22|Lando Calrissian}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|sallazend|Zend, Salla}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Luwingo |es=Luwingo }} [[Category:Cyborgs]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Smugglers]] [[Category:Yaka]]
